description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular body and four slender, angled legs. The front features a large, slightly recessed screen bordered by a thick, light-colored bezel, with a dark panel below the screen displaying a circular emblem. Two long, thin metal antennas extend upward from the top, characteristic of mid-20th-century TV designs. The side panels are dark brown, likely wood or wood veneer, while the back is a lighter color with ventilation grilles and visible connection ports. The right side of the front panel houses a vertical array of control knobs and buttons, typical of analog televisions. The overall appearance suggests a sturdy, utilitarian build with a retro aesthetic.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a large screen, wood-paneled sides, metal antennas, and front control knobs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the screen, thick bezel, emblem, antennas, and control panel on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the body, dark wood panel, and the protruding antennas.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the light-colored back panel with ventilation grilles and connection ports.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the solid wood panel and the profile of the television's body.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, light-colored, likely plastic or wood, with a simple rectangular shape and no visible ornamentation.
attribute_control_button_layout: Vertical row on the front right edge, several knobs and buttons visible.
